title: Describe the purpose, functions, and concepts of first hop redundancy protocols

questions.question: Which protocol is an IEEE standard that is supported openly as a first hop redundancy protocol (FHRP)?

incorrect.explanation: Proxy ARP does not match the mechanism asked here.

questions.question: Which statement is correct about HSRP?
choices: All routers in an HSRP group are active.
Only one router in an HSRP group can be active.
The virtual router sends hello packets to the HSRP group.
HSRP allows for per-packet load balancing.

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "Only one router in an HSRP group can be active.".

incorrect.explanation: All routers in an HSRP group are active. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "All routers in an HSRP group are active." without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: The virtual router sends hello packets to the HSRP group. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "The virtual router sends hello packets t…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: HSRP allows for per-packet load balancing. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "HSRP allows for per-packet load balancin…" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: FHRP gives hosts one virtual default gateway — track Active/Standby (HSRP) vs master/backup (VRRP).

questions.question: Which is a difference between HSRPv1 and HSRPv2?
choices: HSRPv2 does not use hello packets.
HSRPv1 uses broadcasts, and HSRPv2 uses multicasts.

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "HSRPv2 uses milliseconds.".

incorrect.explanation: HSRPv2 does not use hello packets. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "HSRPv2 does not use hello packets." without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: HSRPv1 uses broadcasts, and HSRPv2 uses multicasts. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "HSRPv1 uses broadcasts, and HSRPv2 uses …" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: HSRPv1 supports IPv6. reflects a common trap: IPv6 static routes require ipv6 unicast-routing globally first. Without ipv6 unicast-routing, IPv6 static routes are not installed — a common exam gotcha.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: IPv6 static routes require `ipv6 unicast-routing` globally first

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: IPv6 static routes require ipv6 unicast-routing globally first — Without ipv6 unicast-routing, IPv6 static routes are not installed — a common exam gotcha

questions.question: Which statement is correct about GLBP?
choices: The active router is responsible for responding to clients with the virtual router’s MAC address.
The active virtual gateway will respond with a MAC address of the active router.
The active virtual gateway will respond with a MAC address of an active virtual forwarder.
The virtual router is responsible for responding to tracking requests.

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "The active virtual gateway will respond with a MAC address of an active virtual forwarder.".

incorrect.explanation: The active router is responsible for responding to clients with the virtual router’s MAC address. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "The active router is responsible for res…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: The active virtual gateway will respond with a MAC address of the active router. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "The active virtual gateway will respond …" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: The virtual router is responsible for responding to tracking requests. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "The virtual router is responsible for re…" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: FHRP gives hosts one virtual default gateway — track Active/Standby (HSRP) vs master/backup (VRRP).

incorrect.explanation: The router with the lowest priority and lowest IP address does not match the mechanism asked here.

questions.question: What is the definition of preemption for HSRP?
choices: It allows the protocol to effectively load-balance per packet.
It watches an upstream interface and fails over when the interface goes down.
It ignores the priorities of the routers and elects an active router by highest IP address.
When a standby router comes online, it allows for a re-election of the active router.

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "When a standby router comes online, it allows for a re-election of the active router.".

incorrect.explanation: It allows the protocol to effectively load-balance per packet. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "It allows the protocol to effectively lo…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: It watches an upstream interface and fails over when the interface goes down. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "It watches an upstream interface and fai…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: It ignores the priorities of the routers and elects an active router by highest IP address. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "It ignores the priorities of the routers…" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: FHRP gives hosts one virtual default gateway — track Active/Standby (HSRP) vs master/backup (VRRP).

Configure all routers in the HSRP as active routers.

questions.question: You have just changed the priority on Router A to 150, and it has not become the active router. All other routers have the default priority. What is wrong?
choices: The hold timer is set too high and needs to timeout.
The HSRP group is not set for preemption.
Router A’s IP address is too low.

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "The HSRP group is not set for preemption.".

incorrect.explanation: The default priority is 150. reflects a common trap: Treating the default route as a normal match. 0.0.0.0/0 is the least specific route (prefix length 0) and is used only when nothing more specific matches.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Treating the default route as a normal match

incorrect.explanation: The hold timer is set too high and needs to timeout. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "The hold timer is set too high and needs…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: Router A’s IP address is too low. does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Router A’s IP address is too low." without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: On the exam: Treating the default route as a normal match — 0.0.0.0/0 is the least specific route (prefix length 0) and is used only when nothing more specific matches

questions.question:
HSRP topology:
Router A (active) and Router B (standby) share VIP 192.168.1.254
Both track upstream ISP link on serial 0/0/1 for failover

You are running HSRP on Router A and Router B. You intermittently have ISP outages. What command should you configure to alert HSRP to the outage?
choices: RouterA(config-if)#standby 1 interface tracking serial 0/0/1 RouterB(config-if)#standby 1 interface tracking serial 0/0/1
RouterA(config-if)#standby 1 tracking serial 0/0/1 RouterB(config-if)#standby 1 tracking serial 0/0/1
RouterA(config-if)#standby 1 track serial 0/0/1 RouterB(config-if)#standby 1 track serial 0/0/1
RouterA(config-if)#interface serial 0/0/1 RouterA(config-if)#standby 1 interface tracking RouterA(config-if)#interface serial 0/0/1 RouterB(config-if)#standby 1 interface tracking

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "RouterA(config-if)#standby 1 track serial 0/0/1 RouterB(config-if)#standby 1 track serial 0/0/1".

incorrect.explanation: RouterA(config-if)#standby 1 interface tracking serial 0/0/1 RouterB(config-if)#standby 1 interface tracking serial 0/0/1 does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "RouterA(config-if)#standby 1 interface t…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: RouterA(config-if)#standby 1 tracking serial 0/0/1 RouterB(config-if)#standby 1 tracking serial 0/0/1 does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "RouterA(config-if)#standby 1 tracking se…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: RouterA(config-if)#interface serial 0/0/1 RouterA(config-if)#standby 1 interface tracking RouterA(config-if)#interface serial 0/0/1 RouterB(config-if)#standby 1 interface tracking does not match the mechanism asked here.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "RouterA(config-if)#interface serial 0/0/…" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: HSRP: hosts use virtual IP/MAC; know Active vs Standby and preempt/priority.
